summary: Slower week with only 1 conversion at $226.85 — above our $80 target. The 30-day average ($88.21/conversion) is still near target, so this looks like normal variance on a small-volume campaign. We identified mobile traffic as a confirmed budget drain and have a plan to fix it.

metrics.label: Conversions
metrics.note: Down from ~3/week avg — small sample variance

metrics.label: Avg CPC
metrics.note: 41 clicks, 9.36% CTR — traffic volume is stable

funnel.title: Ad-to-Booking Funnel
funnel.notes: The single conversion came from desktop via 'bartenders for hire' keyword. Mobile clicks (22 of 41) produced zero bookings.

findings.title: Mobile Traffic Is a Confirmed Budget Drain
findings.description: Mobile got 54% of clicks and 37% of spend but produced zero conversions across multiple weeks. We've now increased the mobile bid reduction from -20% to -50% to redirect budget toward desktop where conversions happen.

findings.title: One Bad Week, Not a Broken Campaign
findings.description: The 30-day average is 10 conversions at $88.21 each — still near the $80 target. With only ~3 conversions per week on average, a 1-conversion week is within normal variance for this volume.

findings.title: Single Keyword Carrying the Account
findings.description: 'Bartenders for hire' drives 89% of spend and is the only keyword converting. The other keywords combined generated just 3 clicks. This concentration is a risk if competition increases on that term.

actions.title: Monitored Target CPA Performance
actions.description: The lowered $80 Target CPA has been running for one full week. Early signal: the algorithm is limiting spend rather than finding cheaper conversions. Needs more time before adjusting.

actions.title: Analyzed Device Performance
actions.description: Confirmed the mobile conversion gap is persistent — zero conversions despite significant click volume. A -20% bid adjustment wasn't enough.

actions.title: Increased Mobile Bid Reduction to -50%
actions.description: Mobile was consuming 37% of budget with zero conversions. Increased the bid adjustment from -20% to -50% to shift budget toward desktop where all conversions occur.

nextSteps: Monitor whether the -50% mobile bid reduction shifts budget to desktop and improves conversions
Give the $80 Target CPA one more week — if CPA stays elevated, raise back to $100
Review search terms for any wasted spend or new keyword opportunities
